meta.name: PSS-10 Perceived Stress Scale
meta.description: The PSS-10 is a 10-item self-report scale that measures the degree to which situations in one's life are appraised as stressful. It assesses how unpredictable, uncontrollable, and overloaded respondents find their lives over the past month. It is the most widely used psychological instrument for measuring perceived stress.
meta.author: Cohen, Kamarck & Mermelstein (1983)
meta.reference: Cohen S, Kamarck T, Mermelstein R. A global measure of perceived stress. J Health Soc Behav. 1983;24(4):385-396.
meta.instruction: The questions in this scale ask you about your feelings and thoughts during the last month. In each case, please indicate how often you felt or thought a certain way.

questions.text: In the last month, how often have you been upset because of something that happened unexpectedly?
In the last month, how often have you felt that you were unable to control the important things in your life?
In the last month, how often have you felt nervous and "stressed"?
In the last month, how often have you felt confident about your ability to handle your personal problems?
In the last month, how often have you felt that things were going your way?
In the last month, how often have you found that you could not cope with all the things that you had to do?
In the last month, how often have you been able to control irritations in your life?
In the last month, how often have you felt that you were on top of things?
In the last month, how often have you been angered because of things that happened that were outside of your control?
In the last month, how often have you felt difficulties were piling up so high that you could not overcome them?

ranges.label: Low Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress level is low, indicating that you are managing life demands effectively. You appear to feel in control of important aspects of your life and are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current effective coping strategies and balanced lifestyle habits
Keep up healthy routines including regular exercise, adequate sleep, and social engagement
Practice mindfulness or relaxation techniques proactively to sustain your well-being over time

ranges.label: Moderate Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress is at a moderate level, suggesting that life demands may sometimes feel unpredictable or difficult to manage. Enhanced stress management strategies could help restore a greater sense of control.
suggestions: Identify your primary sources of stress and develop targeted strategies to address them proactively
Practice daily stress management techniques including deep breathing, meditation, or physical exercise
Maintain a balanced work-life schedule with clear boundaries between professional and personal time
Consider speaking with a counselor or therapist if stress feels overwhelming or unmanageable
Prioritize consistent sleep hygiene and regular physical activity as foundational stress buffers

ranges.label: High Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress level is high, indicating that life demands feel substantially overwhelming and uncontrollable. This sustained level of stress may be negatively affecting both your physical and mental health.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional for structured support and evidence-based stress interventions
Implement immediate stress-reduction strategies including regular exercise, social support, and relaxation practices
Review and adjust your current commitments and workload to reduce unnecessary sources of pressure
Practice daily relaxation or mindfulness exercises to counteract the physiological effects of chronic stress
Avoid relying on unhealthy coping mechanisms such as excessive alcohol, caffeine, or social withdrawal
